description_long: This object consists of a group of axes, each featuring a double-edged blade mounted on a wooden handle. The blades are primarily colored in green and gray tones, with some axes having a more pronounced curve and others a straighter edge. The handles are brown, resembling polished wood, and vary in length, with some axes appearing larger and more robust than others. The blades are attached to the handles with visible metal fastenings, and the overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. The axes are arranged in two parallel rows, showcasing both the front and back of the blades. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, emphasizing a practical, functional aesthetic.
description: A group of double-edged axes with green and gray blades mounted on brown wooden handles, arranged in two rows. The axes vary in size and blade curvature, with a utilitarian, functional design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the axes, highlighting the blade thickness and handle alignment.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full face of the blades, revealing the double-edged design and color variations.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the blade thickness and the arrangement of the handles.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the backs of the blades and the curvature of the handles, with the axes' profiles visible.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handles with a rounded cross-section, smooth finish, and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, symmetrical blades with a broad, slightly curved edge and a central mounting eye; no beard or poll, simple utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal fastenings securing the blade to the handle.
